EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER FOR STIPULATED SETTLEMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF KAPLAN, JOSEPH

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT NAJIB YASER'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE JUDGMENT IS DENIED. BASED ON THE EVIDENCE BEFORE THE COURT, NOTICE WAS GIVEN TO THE DEFENDANT. THE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED DID NOT CHANGE THE FACT THAT THERE WAS A SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT ON MAY 29, 2008 GIVING THE PLAINTIFF THE RIGHT OF POSSESSION OF THE ENTIRE PREMISES. AND IT ALSO GAVE THE PLAINTIFF THE ABILITY TO COME ON AN EX-PARTE BASIS TO ENFORCE THE SETTLEMENT WHICH THE PLAINTIFF HAS DONE WITH ADEQUATE PROOF AND THEREFORE, THEY ARE ENTITLED TO RELIEF SOUGHT. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT NAJIB YASER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED 5 DAYS TO ANSWER. THIS IS A GENERAL DEMURRER TO THE ENTIRE COMPLAINT AND THERE IS AT LEAST ONE VIABLE CLAIM. UPON CALLING OF THE CALENDAR THE COURT PASSED THE MATTER TO GIVE THE DEFENDANT AN OPPORTUNITY TO APPEAR. AT 10 AM THE MATTER WAS RE-CALLED. THE DFENDANT DID NOT APPEAR AND THE TENTATIVE RULING WAS ADOPTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189

RETURN OF EXECUTION FOR POSSESSION OF REAL PROPERTY ISSUED TO SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY, RETURNED AND FILED, SATISFIED AS TO REAL PROPERTY ONLY AS TO DEFENDANT YASER, NAJIB

LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT VACATED ITS EARLIER RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT NAJIB YASER'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 4, 2009 FOR HEARING DUE TO CLERICAL ERROR. (ATTY. JEROME J. GILGHIOTTI JR. AND MR. NAJIB YASER WERE INFORMED BY CLERK TO APPEAR IN COURT) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, RULING - THIS MATTER WAS ON THE COURT'S CALENDAR ON JUNE 3, 2009 WITH AN INCORRECT TENTATIVE RULING WHICH STATED IT WAS TAKEN OFF CALENDAR BY THE MOVING PARTY. THE COURT CONTINUED THE MATTER FOR JUNE 4, 2009 IN ORDER TO ALLOW THE PARTIES TO HAVE AN OPPORTUNITY TO ARGUE THE MATTER BEFORE THE COURT. THE CLERK NOTIFIED BOTH PARTIES TO APPEAR IN TODAY'S HEARING. WHEN THE MATTER WAS CALLED AT 10:00 A.M., THERE WAS NO APPEARANCE BY THE DEFENDANT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANT NAJIB YASER'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS DENIED; PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE SHOWS PROPER SUBSTITUTE SERVICE, 5 DAYS TO RESPOND TO THE COMPLAINT. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR # 8189 301/PJB
